name: Satellite Internet Bandwidth per Dollar
description: Satellite internet throughput per dollar of monthly subscription cost. Tracks progression from legacy GEO satellites through LEO mega-constellations (Starlink, OneWeb, Kuiper).

data_points.source: HughesNet Gen3 — ~1.5 Mbps down for ~$60/mo (GEO satellite broadband)
WildBlue / HughesNet Gen4 era — ~5 Mbps for ~$60/mo
ViaSat-1 / HughesNet Gen4 — ~12 Mbps for $60/mo = 0.2, but typical ~5 Mbps usable
ViaSat-2 / HughesNet Jupiter — advertised 25 Mbps for $60/mo but typical real-world ~10 Mbps
Starlink beta — ~50-100 Mbps for $99/mo. Using 50 Mbps conservative avg.
Starlink V2 Mini satellites — median download improving to ~100-120 Mbps for $99/mo
Starlink V2 Mini with direct-to-cell — speeds increasing, ~150 Mbps typical for $99/mo (approx)
